title: Triknda-Yoga: Bhakti Surpasses Knowledge and Detachment
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “S'r Uddhava said: 'The Vedic literature of Your Lordship, oh Lotus-eyed One, that pays attention to the injunctions concerning actions and prohibitions, deals about the good and bad sides…” and develops toward “When one conquers one's breath and senses, one should not forget what the actual purpose of the mind is. With one's intelligence perfect in goodness,…”

This passage begins with “This restraining of the mind with the intimate confidentiality with which one also constantly observes a horse that one wants to tame, is what is known as the most supreme practice of yoga…” and develops toward “Those who follow these methods I have now instructed, achieve the security of My abode in the direct perception of that what is the Absolute Truth.'”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच विधिश् च प्रतिषेधश् च निगमो हीश्वरस्य ते । अवेक्षते ऽरविण्डाक्ष गुणं दोषं च कर्मणाम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca vidhiś ca pratiṣedhaś ca nigamo hīśvarasya te / avekṣate 'raviṇḍākṣa guṇaṃ doṣaṃ ca karmaṇām
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'The Vedic literature of Your Lordship, oh Lotus-eyed One, that pays attention to the injunctions concerning actions and prohibitions, deals about the good and bad sides of karma [akarma and vikarma].

verses.sanskrit: वर्णाश्रम-विकल्पं च प्रतिलोमानुलोमजम् । द्रव्य-देश-वयः-कालान् स्वर्गं नरकम् एव च
verses.transliteration: varṇāśrama-vikalpaṃ ca pratilomānulomajam / dravya-deśa-vayaḥ-kālān svargaṃ narakam eva ca
verses.translation: They also discuss the differences within the varns'rama system wherein the father may be of a higher [anuloma] or a lower [pratiloma] class than the mother, they are about heaven and hell and expound on the subjects of having possessions, one's age, place and time [see also 4.8: 54 and *].

verses.sanskrit: गुण-दोष-भिदा-दृष्टिम् अन्तरेण वचस् तव । निःश्रेयसं कथं नॄणां निषेध-विधि-लक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: guṇa-doṣa-bhidā-dṛṣṭim antareṇa vacas tava / niḥśreyasaṃ kathaṃ nṝṇāṃ niṣedha-vidhi-lakṣaṇam
verses.translation: How can human beings without Your prohibitive and regulatory words concerning final beatitude, tell the difference between virtue and vice [compare 11.19: 40-45]?

verses.sanskrit: पितृ-देव-मनुष्यानां वेदश् चक्षुस् तवेश्वर । श्रेयस् त्व् अनुपलब्धे ऽर्थे साध्य-साधनयोर् अपि
verses.transliteration: pitṛ-deva-manuṣyānāṃ vedaś cakṣus taveśvara / śreyas tv anupalabdhe 'rthe sādhya-sādhanayor api
verses.translation: The Vedic knowledge emanating from You offers the forefathers, the gods as also the human beings a superior eye upon the - not for everyone that evident - meaning of life, what would be the goal, and how we may achieve.

verses.sanskrit: गुण-दोष-भिदा-दृष्टिर् निगमात् ते न हि स्वतः । निगमेनापवादश् च भिदाया इति ह भ्रमः
verses.transliteration: guṇa-doṣa-bhidā-dṛṣṭir nigamāt te na hi svataḥ / nigamenāpavādaś ca bhidāyā iti ha bhramaḥ
verses.translation: The difference between virtue and vice one can see with the help of Your Vedic knowledge and that insight does not arise by itself, but the Vedas also nullify such a difference and thus clearly confuse the issue....'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच योगास् त्रयो मया प्रोक्ता नॄणां श्रेयो-विधित्सया । ज्ञानं कर्म च भक्तिश् च नोपायो ऽन्यो ऽस्ति कुत्रचित्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca yogās trayo mayā proktā nṝṇāṃ śreyo-vidhitsayā / jñānaṃ karma ca bhaktiś ca nopāyo 'nyo 'sti kutracit
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The three ways of yoga I described in My desire to grant human beings the perfection, are the path of philosophy [jna], the path of work [karma] and the path of devotion [bhakti]; no other means can be found [for one's emancipation. See also B.G. contents and triknda].

verses.sanskrit: निर्विण्णानां ज्ञान-योगो न्यासिनाम् इह कर्मसु । तेष्व् अनिर्विण्ण-चित्तानां कर्म-योगस् तु कामिनाम्
verses.transliteration: nirviṇṇānāṃ jñāna-yogo nyāsinām iha karmasu / teṣv anirviṇṇa-cittānāṃ karma-yogas tu kāminām
verses.translation: For those who disgusted with fruitful labor [or sacrifices] forsake such activities, there is the yoga of spiritual knowledge and for those who have not turned themselves consciously away and do feel for material happiness, there is the path of karma-yoga.

verses.sanskrit: यदृच्छया मत्-कथादौ जात-श्रद्धस् तु यः पुमान् । न निर्विण्णो नाति-सक्तो भक्ति-योगो ऽस्य सिद्धि-दः
verses.transliteration: yadṛcchayā mat-kathādau jāta-śraddhas tu yaḥ pumān / na nirviṇṇo nāti-sakto bhakti-yogo 'sya siddhi-daḥ
verses.translation: When it happens that in a person, who is neither averse nor very attached, faith awakens in My narrations and such, the path of bhakti-yoga will bring perfection.

verses.sanskrit: तावत् कर्माणि कुर्वीत न निर्विद्येत यावता । मत्-कथा-श्रवणादौ वा श्रद्धा यावन् न जायते
verses.transliteration: tāvat karmāṇi kurvīta na nirvidyeta yāvatā / mat-kathā-śravaṇādau vā śraddhā yāvan na jāyate
verses.translation: For as long as one is not fed up [with one's material life] and no faith has awakened in My discourses and listening etc. [7.5: 23-24], one will have to continue with one's fruitful labor [see also 1.2: 7, 11.5: 41].

verses.sanskrit: स्व-धर्म-स्थो यजन् यज्ञैर् अनाशीः-काम उद्धव । न याति स्वर्ग-नरकौ यद्य् अन्यन् न समाचरेत्
verses.transliteration: sva-dharma-stho yajan yajñair anāśīḥ-kāma uddhava / na yāti svarga-narakau yady anyan na samācaret
verses.translation: Someone will not go to heaven nor to hell, oh Uddhava, if he holds on to his prescribed duties without deviating and, free from ulterior motives, is of worship with performing sacrifices [see also B.G. 8: 16].

verses.sanskrit: अस्मिंल् लोके वर्तमानः स्व-धर्म-स्थो ऽनघः शुचिः । ज्ञानं विशुद्धम् आप्नोति मद्-भक्तिं वा यदृच्छया
verses.transliteration: asmiṃl loke vartamānaḥ sva-dharma-stho 'naghaḥ śuciḥ / jñānaṃ viśuddham āpnoti mad-bhaktiṃ vā yadṛcchayā
verses.translation: When one, existing in this world, free from sin is situated in one's duties and is pure [in one's motives], one obtains transcendental knowledge and eventually My bhakti [compare 1.5: 23-31].

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्गिणो ऽप्य् एतम् इच्छन्ति लोकं निरयिणस् तथा । साधकं ज्ञान-भक्तिभ्याम् उभयं तद्-असाधकम्
verses.transliteration: svargiṇo 'py etam icchanti lokaṃ nirayiṇas tathā / sādhakaṃ jñāna-bhaktibhyām ubhayaṃ tad-asādhakam
verses.translation: Just as the residents of hell, even the residents of heaven desire for this planet earth that is so conducive to the spiritual knowledge and devotional service, effective neither in hell nor in heaven.

verses.sanskrit: न नरः स्वर्-गतिं काङ्क्षेन् नारकीं वा विचक्षणः । नेमं लोकं च काङ्क्षेत देहावेशात् प्रमाद्यति
verses.transliteration: na naraḥ svar-gatiṃ kāṅkṣen nārakīṃ vā vicakṣaṇaḥ / nemaṃ lokaṃ ca kāṅkṣeta dehāveśāt pramādyati
verses.translation: A wise human being should not strive for hell or heaven, nor for this planet earth, because one gets bewildered when one enters a physical body.

verses.sanskrit: एतद् विद्वान् पुरा मृत्योर् अभवाय घटेत सः । अप्रमत्त इदं ज्ञात्वा मर्त्यम् अप्य् अर्थ-सिद्धि-दम्
verses.transliteration: etad vidvān purā mṛtyor abhavāya ghaṭeta saḥ / apramatta idaṃ jñātvā martyam apy artha-siddhi-dam
verses.translation: Even though [the body is] subject to death, it brings the perfection of one's life purpose within reach. Someone who knows this should [therefore], before he dies, attentively endeavor for transcendence.

verses.sanskrit: छिद्यमानं यमैर् एतैः कृत-नीडं वनस्पतिम् । खगः स्व-केतम् उत्सृज्य क्षेमं याति ह्य् अलम्पटः
verses.transliteration: chidyamānaṃ yamair etaiḥ kṛta-nīḍaṃ vanaspatim / khagaḥ sva-ketam utsṛjya kṣemaṃ yāti hy alampaṭaḥ
verses.translation: Not being attached a bird attains happiness after giving up the nest it made in a tree that was cut down by some messengers of death.

verses.sanskrit: अहो-रात्रैश् छिद्यमानं बुद्ध्वायुर् भय-वेपथुः । मुक्त-सङ्गः परं बुद्ध्वा निरीह उपशाम्यति
verses.transliteration: aho-rātraiś chidyamānaṃ buddhvāyur bhaya-vepathuḥ / mukta-saṅgaḥ paraṃ buddhvā nirīha upaśāmyati
verses.translation: Knowing that with every day and night one's life span is shortened [likewise], one is beset with fear; free from attachment [though] one, with the intelligence of transcendence being without desires, attains perfect peace.

verses.sanskrit: नृ-देहम् आद्यं सु-लभं सु-दुर्लभं प्लवं सु-कल्पं गुरु-कर्णधारम् मयानुकूलेन नभस्वतेरितं पुमान् भवाब्धिं न तरेत् स आत्म-हा
verses.transliteration: nṛ-deham ādyaṃ su-labhaṃ su-durlabhaṃ $ plavaṃ su-kalpaṃ guru-karṇadhāram &mayānukūlena nabhasvateritaṃ % pumān bhavābdhiṃ na taret sa ātma-hā
verses.translation: A person is the killer of his own soul when he, in this well suited boat of the so rarely attained but naturally acquired physical human body, does not cross over the ocean of material existence, being propelled by the favorable winds that I am, and with the spiritual master as the captain.

verses.sanskrit: यदारम्भेषु निर्विण्णो विरक्तः संयतेन्द्रियः । अभ्यासेनात्मनो योगी धारयेद् अचलं मनः
verses.transliteration: yadārambheṣu nirviṇṇo viraktaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ / abhyāsenātmano yogī dhārayed acalaṃ manaḥ
verses.translation: When a yogi, disgusted with material endeavoring, being detached is in full control of his senses, he has to concentrate his mind to stabilize it with self-discipline.

verses.sanskrit: धार्यमाणं मनो यर्हि भ्राम्यद् अश्व् अनवस्थितम् । अतन्द्रितो ऽनुरोधेन मार्गेणात्म-वशं नयेत्
verses.transliteration: dhāryamāṇaṃ mano yarhi bhrāmyad aśv anavasthitam / atandrito 'nurodhena mārgeṇātma-vaśaṃ nayet
verses.translation: The mind being narrowed down to the spiritual platform should, when suddenly being drawn away, carefully - according to the rules of the game - be brought under the control of the self [see also B.G. 6: 26].

verses.sanskrit: मनो-गतिं न विसृजेज् जित-प्राणो जितेन्द्रियः । सत्त्व-सम्पन्नया बुद्ध्या मन आत्म-वशं नयेत्
verses.transliteration: mano-gatiṃ na visṛjej jita-prāṇo jitendriyaḥ / sattva-sampannayā buddhyā mana ātma-vaśaṃ nayet
verses.translation: When one conquers one's breath and senses, one should not forget what the actual purpose of the mind is. With one's intelligence perfect in goodness, it should always be led back under the supervision of the soul [to be its servant, see B.G. 3: 42].

verses.sanskrit: एष वै परमो योगो मनसः सङ्ग्रहः स्मृतः । हृदय-ज्ञत्वम् अन्विच्छन् दम्यस्येवार्वतो मुहुः
verses.transliteration: eṣa vai paramo yogo manasaḥ saṅgrahaḥ smṛtaḥ / hṛdaya-jñatvam anvicchan damyasyevārvato muhuḥ
verses.translation: This restraining of the mind with the intimate confidentiality with which one also constantly observes a horse that one wants to tame, is what is known as the most supreme practice of yoga [see also B.G. 6: 33-34].

verses.sanskrit: साङ्ख्येन सर्व-भावानां प्रतिलोमानुलोमतः । भवाप्ययाव् अनुध्यायेन् मनो यावत् प्रसीदति
verses.transliteration: sāṅkhyena sarva-bhāvānāṃ pratilomānulomataḥ / bhavāpyayāv anudhyāyen mano yāvat prasīdati
verses.translation: By analyzing how all the different elements [of matter and the fields and principles of spiritual knowledge] cohere and are in conflict, how they originate and how they are lost, the mind should be kept attentive until [spiritually] satisfied.

verses.sanskrit: निर्विण्णस्य विरक्तस्य पुरुषस्योक्त-वेदिनः । मनस् त्यजति दौरात्म्यं चिन्तितस्यानुचिन्तया
verses.transliteration: nirviṇṇasya viraktasya puruṣasyokta-vedinaḥ / manas tyajati daurātmyaṃ cintitasyānucintayā
verses.translation: The mind of someone who, guided by the instructions [of the spiritual master], being disgusted got detached, will by analysis of and meditation on what is perceived, give up its false identification [with matter and the body].

verses.sanskrit: यमादिभिर् योग-पथैर् आन्वीक्षिक्या च विद्यया । ममार्चोपासनाभिर् वा नान्यैर् योग्यं स्मरेन् मनः
verses.transliteration: yamādibhir yoga-pathair ānvīkṣikyā ca vidyayā / mamārcopāsanābhir vā nānyair yogyaṃ smaren manaḥ
verses.translation: The mind must not be focussed on any other practice than on the austerities and procedures of the [eightfold] yoga method, on logical analyses and spiritual knowledge, or on the exercises of respect for My form [thus on triknda, threefold yoga: karma-, jna- or bhakti-yoga].

verses.sanskrit: यदि कुर्यात् प्रमादेन योगी कर्म विगर्हितम् । योगेनैव दहेद् अंहो नान्यत् तत्र कदाचन
verses.transliteration: yadi kuryāt pramādena yogī karma vigarhitam / yogenaiva dahed aṃho nānyat tatra kadācana
verses.translation: When a yogi out of negligence commits a reprehensible deed, he should burn away that sin by means of the yoga method only; never at any time he should try this a different way [compare B.G. 1.5: 17, 4: 19, 9: 30].

verses.sanskrit: स्वे स्वे ऽधिकारे या निष्ठा स गुणः परिकीर्तितः । कर्मणां जात्य्-अशुद्धानाम् अनेन नियमः कृतः । गुण-दोष-विधानेन सङ्गानां त्याजनेच्छया
verses.transliteration: sve sve 'dhikāre yā niṣṭhā sa guṇaḥ parikīrtitaḥ / karmaṇāṃ jāty-aśuddhānām anena niyamaḥ kṛtaḥ / guṇa-doṣa-vidhānena saṅgānāṃ tyājanecchayā
verses.translation: When each is firmly grounded in his own position one speaks of virtue, but because of the nature of karmic activities one is impure in one's engagement. Driven by desire to put an end to those [impurities or destabilizing] attachments, one consequently with this rule of virtue and vice [by Me, then] arrives at [the needed niyama] regulations.

verses.sanskrit: जात-श्रद्धो मत्-कथासु निर्विण्णः सर्व-कर्मसु । वेद दुःखात्मकान् कामान् परित्यागे ऽप्य् अनीश्वरः
verses.transliteration: jāta-śraddho mat-kathāsu nirviṇṇaḥ sarva-karmasu / veda duḥkhātmakān kāmān parityāge 'py anīśvaraḥ
verses.translation: When faith in my narrations and disgust with all karma has awakened in someone, he [the tmnandi bhakta], knows that his lusts are the root of his misery, despite his lack of ability to [fully] renounce them. Even though sometimes being engaged in them, he should remain happy and resolute in that faith worship Me, repenting the sense gratification that makes him unhappy.

verses.sanskrit: ततो भजेत मां प्रीतः श्रद्धालुर् दृढ-निश्चयः । जुषमाणश् च तान् कामान् दुःखोदर्कांश् च गर्हयन्
verses.transliteration: tato bhajeta māṃ prītaḥ śraddhālur dṛḍha-niścayaḥ / juṣamāṇaś ca tān kāmān duḥkhodarkāṃś ca garhayan
verses.translation: When faith in my narrations and disgust with all karma has awakened in someone, he [the tmnandi bhakta], knows that his lusts are the root of his misery, despite his lack of ability to [fully] renounce them. Even though sometimes being engaged in them, he should remain happy and resolute in that faith worship Me, repenting the sense gratification that makes him unhappy.

verses.sanskrit: प्रोक्तेन भक्ति-योगेन भजतो मासकृन् मुनेः । कामा हृदय्या नश्यन्ति सर्वे मयि हृदि स्थिते
verses.transliteration: proktena bhakti-yogena bhajato māsakṛn muneḥ / kāmā hṛdayyā naśyanti sarve mayi hṛdi sthite
verses.translation: All the lusts a devotee has in his heart, are destroyed when his heart is firmly established in Me by constantly worshiping Me in the bhakti-yoga as described [see sthita-praja].

verses.sanskrit: भिद्यते हृदय-ग्रन्थिश् छिद्यन्ते सर्व-संशयाः । क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि मयि दृष्टे ऽखिलात्मनि
verses.transliteration: bhidyate hṛdaya-granthiś chidyante sarva-saṃśayāḥ / kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi mayi dṛṣṭe 'khilātmani
verses.translation: The knots in the heart are cut, all misgivings are in shreds and the chain of fruitful actions ends when one sees Me as the Supreme Soul of All.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान् मद्-भक्ति-युक्तस्य योगिनो वै मद्-आत्मनः । न ज्ञानं न च वैराग्यं प्रायः श्रेयो भवेद् इह
verses.transliteration: tasmān mad-bhakti-yuktasya yogino vai mad-ātmanaḥ / na jñānaṃ na ca vairāgyaṃ prāyaḥ śreyo bhaved iha
verses.translation: For a yogi connected in My devotional service who fixed his mind on Me, for that reason generally neither the path of knowledge nor the path of detachment [from fruitful activities] is the way to become happy in this world.

verses.sanskrit: यत् कर्मभिर् यत् तपसा ज्ञान-वैराग्यतश् च यत् । योगेन दान-धर्मेण श्रेयोभिर् इतरैर् अपि
verses.transliteration: yat karmabhir yat tapasā jñāna-vairāgyataś ca yat / yogena dāna-dharmeṇa śreyobhir itarair api
verses.translation: When one happens to desire heaven, final beatitude or My abode, that what is obtained by fruitful action, penance, the cultivation of knowledge and detachment, that what is achieved by mystical yoga, charity, religious observances, auspicious actions or other methods, will by My devotee easily be achieved in being engaged in My bhakti.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं मद्-भक्ति-योगेन मद्-भक्तो लभते ऽञ्जसा । स्वर्गापवर्गं मद्-धाम कथञ्चिद् यदि वाञ्छति
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ mad-bhakti-yogena mad-bhakto labhate 'ñjasā / svargāpavargaṃ mad-dhāma kathañcid yadi vāñchati
verses.translation: When one happens to desire heaven, final beatitude or My abode, that what is obtained by fruitful action, penance, the cultivation of knowledge and detachment, that what is achieved by mystical yoga, charity, religious observances, auspicious actions or other methods, will by My devotee easily be achieved in being engaged in My bhakti.

verses.sanskrit: न किञ्चित् साधवो धीरा भक्ता ह्य् एकान्तिनो मम । वाञ्छन्त्य् अपि मया दत्तं कैवल्यम् अपुनर्-भवम्
verses.transliteration: na kiñcit sādhavo dhīrā bhaktā hy ekāntino mama / vāñchanty api mayā dattaṃ kaivalyam apunar-bhavam
verses.translation: Saintly persons well versed and skillful, devotees fully dedicated to Me, never desire anything but Me, I who grant them beatitude and freedom from birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: नैरपेक्ष्यं परं प्राहुर् निःश्रेयसम् अनल्पकम् । तस्मान् निराशिषो भक्तिर् निरपेक्षस्य मे भवेत्
verses.transliteration: nairapekṣyaṃ paraṃ prāhur niḥśreyasam analpakam / tasmān nirāśiṣo bhaktir nirapekṣasya me bhavet
verses.translation: It is said that it is best not to desire anything, so that with the one who does not seek any personal gain, that with someone who is fully independent, the bhakti unto Me may arise that grants the greatest happiness [see also 2.3: 10].

verses.sanskrit: न मय्य् एकान्त-भक्तानां गुण-दोषोद्भवा गुणाः । साधूनां सम-चित्तानां बुद्धेः परम् उपेयुषाम्
verses.transliteration: na mayy ekānta-bhaktānāṃ guṇa-doṣodbhavā guṇāḥ / sādhūnāṃ sama-cittānāṃ buddheḥ param upeyuṣām
verses.translation: With Me the unfavorable qualities of the modes of nature [like bewilderment, slowness and restlessness] cannot [again] manifest themselves in pure devotees, because they, free from desire, under all circumstances are stable in their consciousness. They now belong to those who moved beyond that what can be understood with a materially motivated intelligence [see also B.G. 9: 30].

verses.sanskrit: एवम् एतान् मया दिष्टान् अनुतिष्ठन्ति मे पथः । क्षेमं विन्दन्ति मत्-स्थानं यद् ब्रह्म परमं विदुः
verses.transliteration: evam etān mayā diṣṭān anutiṣṭhanti me pathaḥ / kṣemaṃ vindanti mat-sthānaṃ yad brahma paramaṃ viduḥ
verses.translation: Those who follow these methods I have now instructed, achieve the security of My abode in the direct perception of that what is the Absolute Truth.'
